name: Pontianak
alias: also Kuntilanak · the woman who died in childbirth
body: She announces herself by scent before sight — frangipani, sweet and too heavy, the smell of a grave dressed in flowers. They say a woman who dies in childbirth, or carrying a child, returns this way: beautiful from the front in a white dress with hair to the waist, ruined from behind, a hollow in her back.

The cry gives the rule that keeps you alive. When her wail sounds faint and far, she is close. When it is loud, she has already passed you by. She is drawn to the smell of laundry hung out overnight and to new fathers, and she is said to nest in the banana trees, which is why some still tie a red thread around the trunk.
note: The old ward is a nail or sharp iron driven into the hollow at the nape of her neck — do this and she is said to become a dutiful wife until the nail is ever pulled free. Bukit Brown and the older kampong land carry the most sightings.
